english: 1. also sog (sog), γsob (gsob), γsog (gsog), null, void, vain, empty, bad in its quality, not durable. -- 2. also γsob (gsob) something stuffed (as a chair), naṅ-sob (nang sob), k'óṅ-sob (khong sob), k'óg-sob (khog sob) Wdn. prob. id.; sob-stán (sob stan) cushion, bolster, mattress, pags-sób (pags sob) the stuffed skin of an animal, seṅ(-gei) sób (seng gei sob) the stuffed skin of a lion Pth.
1. also γsom (gsom), sóm-śiṅ (som shing) fir-tree, pine-tree. -- 2. also soms (soms), imp. of sém-pa (sem pa).

1. also γsor (gsor), gimlet, rús-pa .búgs-pai sor (rus pa 'bugs pai sor) S.g. prob. a sort of trephine. -- 2. v. sór-mo (sor mo). -- 3. v. sar (sar), sor bźag-pa (sor bzhag pa) to put in its place (Sch. also: 'quite the same'?); raṅ-sor (rang sor) v. raṅ (rang) compounds. -- 4. (cognate to só-ma (so ma)?) sor ćud-pa (sor chud pa) (Sch. .jud-pa ('jud pa)) to restore, renew, e.g. exhausted strength Dzl., the doctrine of Buddha Pth. -- 5. term. of so (so).

resp. p'yag-sór (phyag sor) Mil., źabs-sór (zhabs sor), 1. finger, toe; sor-γdúb (sor gdub) finger-ring, sor-tsigs (sor tshigs) the joint of a finger. -- 2. inch, sor-bźi-pa (sor bzhi pa) four-inched.
coal, esp. charcoal, = sol-nág (sol nag); sól-bai me (sol bai me) coal-fire Lt.; *sol-mé* (*sol me*) W. live coal, burning coal.
resp. friendly, kind, affable C., W.
1. inst. of so (so), sos btúb-pa (sos btub pa) to bite, Sch. also to backbite, to calumniate. -- 2. v. γsó-ba (gso ba) and .ts'ó-ba ('tsho ba).
1. in Tibet: spring, = dpýid (dpyid), Mil. -- 2. in India: the hot season, from about the middle of April till the middle of June.
disease of the membrum virile, in five forms (prob. differ-ent stages of gonorrhea) Mng.

1. adj., also srá-bo (sra bo) and srá-mo (sra mo) Cs., col. *srán-te* (*sran te*) (cf. srán-pa (sran pa)), hard, solid, compact, firm, and abstract noun: solidity, hardness, compactness, of wood, meat etc., and often fig.: γźu-srán (gzhu sran) a bow difficult to be bent S.g., rgas-srá (rgas sra) hearty vigorous old age S.g.; mts'on k'ar sra (mtshon khar sra) proof against cut and thrust, also: proof against malicious words Mil. -- sra-brkyaṅ (sra brkyang), the coarse blanket of a monk. -- sa-rtsí (sa rtsi) varnish. -- 2. vb. W., to empty.

I. (cf. sróṅ-ba (srong ba)) 1. pair of scales, balance B., sraṅ-la .dégs-pa (srang la 'degs pa) Cs., γźál-ba (gzhal ba), ts'ád-pa (tshad pa) Sch., sráṅ-ba (srang ba) Sp. to weigh, to balance. -- 2. steel-yard, *sraṅ tág-će* (*srang thag ce*) to hold the steel-yard, in weighing. -- 3. weight, in a general sense, bre - sraṅ (bre srang) weight and measure, rgya-sraṅ (rgya srang) Chinese weights Cs. -- 4. an ounce, sraṅ gáṅ (srang gang) one ounce, sraṅ do (srang do) two ounces, sman sraṅ ༣༣ (sman srang 33), two pounds of medicine, the daily quantity taken by Buddha when he had caught cold Dzl. 42, 3. -- sráṅ-ća (srang cha) Sch. balance and what belongs to it. -- sraṅ-mdá (srang mda') scale-beam or lever of a pair of scales Sch. -- sraṅ - pór (srang phor) scale. -- sraṅ-tsád (srang tshad) Cs. weight. -- sráṅ-ba (srang ba) vb. v. above. II. street, lam-sráṅ (lam srang) id.; sraṅ-yár (srang yar) Sch.: tortuous path, labyrinth (?). -- rgya-sráṅ (rgya srang) street, lane Glr.; rgyu-sráṅ (rgyu srang) the road which a person habitually walks.

(cf. srá-ba (sra ba)) Cs.: pf. fut. bsran (bsran), imp. sron (sron), W. *srán-će* (*sran ce*) to suffer, bear (with patience), endure, to be hardened W. frq., *srán-t'ub-k'an* (*sran thub khan*) or *srán-t'eg-k'an* (*sran theg khan*) one that can endure much; *k'yod sran gos* (*khyod sran gos*) Ld. you must hold out, you must stand it; in B. sran .dzugs-pa (sran 'dzugs pa) is used in the same sense; *k'yág-sran-ćan* (*khyag sran can*) hardened, accustomed to frost, *dúg-sran-ćan* (*dug sran can*) inured to hardships W. -- *srán-te* (*sran te*) col. frq. adj.: 1. = srá-ba (sra ba) (opp. to lhód-po (lhod po) and .ból-mo ('bol mo)) hard, firm, durable, rigid, strict. 2. fig. hard, severe, bitter. -- sdug-srán (sdug sran) hardiness Mil.; sran-ćé-ba (sran che ba) = srán-t'ub-mk'an (sran thub mkhan). Cs.: srán-pa (sran pa) sbst. hardship, severe distress or toil, srán-par (sran par) toilsomely, rigorously, srán-pa-po (sran pa po) one that hardens himself (?).

1. pease, beans, lentils, Cs. mentions also srad-dkár (srad dkar), srad-nág (srad nag) and srad-sṅón (srad sngon), also mkál-srán (mkal sran), in W., however, we only met with the common field pea and some dry imported Indian sorts of it (mon-srán (mon sran)); rgya-srán (rgya sran) (Cs. mon-srán (mon sran)) was the name the
